sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: West Ham United have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches with 1 win, 2 draws, and 2 losses, scoring an average of 1.4 goals per game. Nottingham Forest are also struggling, with no wins in their last 4 matches (2 draws, 2 losses), conceding an average of 1.8 goals per game. This suggests both teams are in a rough patch, but Forest's defensive vulnerabilities are more pronounced.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two encounters, Nottingham Forest have dominated West Ham, winning 3-0 and 2-0, showcasing their ability to exploit West Ham's defensive frailties. West Ham have failed to score in both matches, indicating a psychological edge for Forest in direct matchups.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Injuries
sections.text: West Ham's Jarrod Bowen is in form with 3 goals in the last 5 games, while Forest's Chris Wood remains a threat with 20 goals this season. West Ham are missing Michail Antonio, impacting their attack, and Forest are without key defender Murillo, potentially weakening their backline.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: West Ham rank 15th in goals scored and conceded, struggling with consistency (42 goals scored, 59 conceded). Nottingham Forest, positioned 7th, have a better offensive record (56 goals scored) but have defensive issues (44 conceded), which could lead to an open game with chances for both sides.

sections.subtitle: Match Context and Trends
sections.text: Historical data suggests matches between these teams often see over 2.5 goals (7/9 head-to-heads) and high corner counts (over 10.5 in 4/5). With Forest's recent inability to keep clean sheets (7 games) and West Ham's tendency for both teams to score (5/6), a goal-heavy game is likely.
